headline: Stock Futures Slide With Markets on Edge Ahead of Big Tech Earnings
summary: Stocks were on course to open a touch lower on Monday, with investors seemingly reluctant to make any major moves ahead of a slew of Big Tech earnings and a Federal Reserve interest-rate decision.  S&P 500 futures slipped 0.1% and contracts tied to the tech-heavy Nasdaq 100 were flat.  Both the S&P and the Nasdaq hit record closing highs at the end of last week, powered higher by Intel and other chip stocks.

S&P 500 futures slipped 0.1% and contracts tied to the tech-heavy Nasdaq 100 were flat.  Both the S&P and the Nasdaq hit record closing highs at the end of last week, powered higher by Intel and other chip stocks.

headline: China blocks Meta's acquisition of AI firm Manus
summary: China has blocked Meta's acquisition of AI startup Manus, the top economic planning body said Monday, after a regulatory review that reportedly also saw Beijing restrict two co-founders from leaving the country.Chief executive Xiao Hong and chief scientist Ji Yichao, who are usually based in Singapore, were reportedly summoned to a meeting in Beijing in March and told they were not allowed to leave China because of a regulatory review of the Meta acquisition.

content.title: China blocks Meta's acquisition of AI firm Manus
content.summary: China has blocked Meta's acquisition of AI startup Manus, the top economic planning body said Monday, after a regulatory review that reportedly also saw Beijing restrict two co-founders from leaving the country.Chief executive Xiao Hong and chief scientist Ji Yichao, who are usually based in Singapore, were reportedly summoned to a meeting in Beijing in March and told they were not allowed to leave China because of a regulatory review of the Meta acquisition.

thumbnail.caption: Beijing has blocked Meta's acquisition of Manus, an artificial intelligence agent created by a company founded in China but now based in Singapore (ADEK BERRY)

headline: Meta partners with space startup Overview Energy to secure solar power for data centers
summary: Meta Platforms has signed an agreement with Overview Energy to secure power from the startup's space-based ‌solar energy infrastructure for the Facebook parent's data centers by ‌the end of the decade.  Overview Energy is developing a system that can collect ​solar energy in space and beam it to facilities on the ground for power generation around the clock.  Initial orbital demonstration of the system is expected in 2028, with commercial power delivery in 2030, the ‌companies said.

content.title: Meta partners with space startup Overview Energy to secure solar power for data centers
content.summary: Meta Platforms has signed an agreement with Overview Energy to secure power from the startup's space-based ‌solar energy infrastructure for the Facebook parent's data centers by ‌the end of the decade.  Overview Energy is developing a system that can collect ​solar energy in space and beam it to facilities on the ground for power generation around the clock.  Initial orbital demonstration of the system is expected in 2028, with commercial power delivery in 2030, the ‌companies said.

headline: China Halts Meta’s $2 Billion Deal for Manus
summary: Chinese authorities have stepped in to stop Meta Platforms Inc. (NASDAQ:META) from proceeding with its proposed $2 billion acquisition of Manus, according to a statement released Monday by the National Development and Reform Commission.

content.title: China Halts Meta’s $2 Billion Deal for Manus
content.summary: Chinese authorities have stepped in to stop Meta Platforms Inc. (NASDAQ:META) from proceeding with its proposed $2 billion acquisition of Manus, according to a statement released Monday by the National Development and Reform Commission.

thumbnail.caption: Meta ©Shutterstock

headline: China blocks Meta from acquiring AI startup Manus
summary: China on Monday blocked Meta’s acquisition of the artificial intelligence startup Manus, which has Chinese roots but is Singapore-based.  In a short statement, China’s National Development and Reform Commission, the country's top planning agency, said it was prohibiting a foreign acquisition of Manus and had required all the parties to withdraw from the deal.  It did not specifically name Meta, which owns Facebook and Instagram.

content.title: China blocks Meta from acquiring AI startup Manus
content.summary: China on Monday blocked Meta’s acquisition of the artificial intelligence startup Manus, which has Chinese roots but is Singapore-based.  In a short statement, China’s National Development and Reform Commission, the country's top planning agency, said it was prohibiting a foreign acquisition of Manus and had required all the parties to withdraw from the deal.  It did not specifically name Meta, which owns Facebook and Instagram.

headline: Stocks Are Wavering to Kick Off a Busy Week
summary: Stocks wavered on Monday to kick off the most packed week in months on Wall Street’s calendar.  “Despite bouts of volatility, for most of this month the showdown between geopolitical uncertainty and enthusiasm over AI-driven earnings growth has been a one-sided battle,” writes Chris Larkin, managing director, trading and investing at E*TRADE from Morgan Stanley.  “This week could show whether the bulls’ enthusiasm has been misplaced,” writes Larkin.

headline: Meta Deal for Manus Blocked by Beijing. Why AI Start-Up Is a Victim of U.S.-China Tension.
summary: Meta Platforms  has been ordered to unwind its acquisition of artificial-intelligence start-up Manus by Chinese authorities.  China’s National Development and Reform Commission said Monday that it had ordered the $2.5 billion acquisition be rescinded on national security grounds.  Meta didn’t immediately respond to a request for comment from Barron’s. A spokesperson for the company said the transaction complied with applicable law and Meta expects an “appropriate resolution,” The Wall Street Journal reported.

content.title: Meta Deal for Manus Blocked by Beijing. Why AI Start-Up Is a Victim of U.S.-China Tension.
content.summary: Meta Platforms  has been ordered to unwind its acquisition of artificial-intelligence start-up Manus by Chinese authorities.  China’s National Development and Reform Commission said Monday that it had ordered the $2.5 billion acquisition be rescinded on national security grounds.  Meta didn’t immediately respond to a request for comment from Barron’s. A spokesperson for the company said the transaction complied with applicable law and Meta expects an “appropriate resolution,” The Wall Street Journal reported.

headline: The Stock Market’s AI Rally Will Get a Test From Apple, Big Tech Earnings
summary: U.S. stocks are on an impressive run heading into this week’s slate of earnings, with an index of semiconductor names notching its longest winning streak on record, the Magnificent Seven tech giants rising nearly 20% since late March, and the broader  notching a series of record highs last week that pegs the benchmark well north of the 7000-point mark.  Communication services, information technology, and consumer discretionary, the three sectors that house the Mag Seven stocks and have driven around 60% of the market’s post-March rebound, are expected to contribute around 47% of S&P 500 earnings over the first quarter.

content.title: The Stock Market’s AI Rally Will Get a Test From Apple, Big Tech Earnings
content.summary: U.S. stocks are on an impressive run heading into this week’s slate of earnings, with an index of semiconductor names notching its longest winning streak on record, the Magnificent Seven tech giants rising nearly 20% since late March, and the broader  notching a series of record highs last week that pegs the benchmark well north of the 7000-point mark.  Communication services, information technology, and consumer discretionary, the three sectors that house the Mag Seven stocks and have driven around 60% of the market’s post-March rebound, are expected to contribute around 47% of S&P 500 earnings over the first quarter.

headline: Meta Secures Space-Based Solar Energy Deal to Power AI Data Centers
summary: Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META) announced Monday that it has entered into an agreement to purchase up to 1 gigawatt of space-based solar energy from Overview Energy, a company developing satellite technology to harvest sunlight in orbit and transmit it back to Earth as electricity. The planned capacity is roughly equivalent to the output of a nuclear reactor.

The planned capacity is roughly equivalent to the output of a nuclear reactor.

headline: China Blocks Meta Manus Deal As AI Ambitions Meet Regulatory Limits
summary: Chinese regulators have blocked Meta Platforms' planned $2b acquisition of AI startup Manus. Authorities have reportedly ordered Meta to unwind the deal, directly affecting its AI agent plans. The decision highlights growing regulatory intervention in cross border AI transactions involving U.S. tech companies. For investors watching NasdaqGS:META, this development lands at a time when the stock is trading at $675.03, with a 1 year return of 23.2% and a 3 year return of 179.8%. The company...

headline: Why Meta’s Space-Based Solar Pact Is Really a Rocket Lab Story
summary: Meta Platforms’ (NASDAQ: META) pact to explore space-based solar power for its data center fleet is fundamentally a launch story. Beaming gigawatt-scale energy from orbit only works if you can deploy massive arrays cheaply and frequently, which puts the spotlight on launch economics and small-sat platforms. Several public companies sit somewhere on this stack. Here we ... Why Meta’s Space-Based Solar Pact Is Really a Rocket Lab Story

headline: At $265: How Amazon Stock Is Getting Rerated
summary: Amazon (AMZN) stock recently crossed its 52-week high to trade near $265, posting a 16% year-to-date gain. At that level, the 34x forward multiple leads many retail investors to conclude the stock has become expensive, typically by comparing it to its four-year historical forward average of roughly 29x. That comparison is the wrong benchmark for where Amazon stands today. The premium to its historical average is not a warning sign but a re-rating, as the market correctly prices in Amazon Web Ser

content.summary: Amazon (AMZN) stock recently crossed its 52-week high to trade near $265, posting a 16% year-to-date gain. At that level, the 34x forward multiple leads many retail investors to conclude the stock has become expensive, typically by comparing it to its four-year historical forward average of roughly 29x.
